title: Central Limit Theorem for Bosonic Quantum Channels
authors: Hami Mehrabi, Ludovico Lami, Mark M. Wilde
abstract: In this paper, we develop an extension of the Central Limit Theorem (CLT) to the setting of bosonic quantum channels. This extension provides a deeper understanding of Gaussian bosonic channels as extremal objects. Using our CLT for bosonic quantum channels, we recover both the classical CLT and the CLT for bosonic quantum states, thereby offering a unified perspective that connects classical probability theory with continuous-variable quantum systems. Moreover, using our result, we can provide necessary uncertainty relations that every physical (possibly non-Gaussian) bosonic quantum channel must satisfy. As another application of our limit theorems, we derive tight lower bounds on the energy-constrained quantum capacity of linear bosonic channels by relating it to the capacity of their associated Gaussian bosonic channels, further reinforcing the role of Gaussian channels as extremal.
summary: This paper develops a Central Limit Theorem for bosonic quantum channels, showing that repeated symmetric convolutions of a channel converge to a Gaussian channel. This result unifies classical and quantum limit theorems and allows for the derivation of new uncertainty relations and tight lower bounds on the quantum capacity of linear bosonic channels.
main_problem: The paper aims to extend the Central Limit Theorem (CLT) from classical probability and quantum states to the setting of bosonic quantum channels, establishing Gaussian channels as extremal objects.
main_result: The authors prove that the n-fold symmetric convolution of a bosonic channel converges to a Gaussian bosonic channel, providing a unified framework that recovers both the classical CLT and the CLT for bosonic states.
method: The study uses the Heisenberg picture, Weyl representation, and n-beam splitter unitaries to define a symmetric convolution of channels, analyzing convergence via the pointwise convergence of characteristic functions.
model_or_system: The system consists of single-mode and multi-mode bosonic quantum systems (continuous-variable systems) described by quadrature operators and canonical commutation relations, specifically focusing on linear bosonic channels.
key_observables: First-order moments (displacement), second-order moments (covariance matrix), characteristic functions, and energy-constrained quantum capacity.
important_parameters: The n-fold convolution limit (n to infinity), energy-constrained regime (E >= 0), and the scaling function of the channel.
main_assumptions: The results assume finite second-order moments for the channel, finite first-order moments, and specifically rely on the linearity of the channel for the capacity lower bound.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a schematic diagram illustrating the construction of the symmetric convolution of a channel using an n-beam splitter unitary.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of extending the CLT to channels, defines the symmetric convolution mechanism, proves the convergence to Gaussian channels (the qCLT for channels), demonstrates the recovery of classical and state-based CLTs, and applies the result to derive uncertainty relations and capacity lower bounds.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for researchers in quantum optics and open quantum systems as it establishes Gaussian channels as the fundamental limit of repeated channel applications and provides new tools for bounding the capacity of continuous-variable quantum communication.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
